title: Cleaning Up a Biohazardous Spill in a Biosafety Cabinet
author: Steven W Wilhelm
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.wghfbt6
materials: Segregate contaminated cleanup materials into the appropriate biohazardous waste containers.

steps.title: Let the BSC run
steps.instruction: Let the BSC run. Do not turn off .

steps.title: Remove broken glass with forceps, tweezers...
steps.instruction: Remove broken glass with forceps, tweezers or other tools and place glass in a sharps container. Do not wipe up broken glass.

steps.title: Pour (don’t spray) disinfectant to contaminated...
steps.instruction: Pour (don’t spray) disinfectant to contaminated surface by pouring it around the periphery of the covered spill moving inward. Allow the appropriate contact time for the disinfectant and agent.

steps.title: After the contact time, wipe up the spilled material
steps.instruction: After the contact time, wipe up the spilled material.

steps.title: Reapply disinfectant to the affected area...
steps.instruction: Reapply disinfectant to the affected area and after the appropriate contact time, wipe up the area. Repeat if necessary.
